summary: Phase 1+2+3 of access-management redesign verified. Backend: 26/26 tests pass in test_permission_catalog_phase1.py (12) + test_custom_roles_phase2.py (14). Permission catalog returns the 11 modules + 117 permissions with labels and sensitivity sort; effective-permissions explain=true returns plain-English summary; roles preview endpoint returns explanation.summary for empty / common / sensitive permission sets; custom roles CRUD works (create/clone/patch/delete + force), system roles are locked (409), empty permissions rejected (400), user_count + is_custom shape returned, session epoch bumps on role update and force-delete all green. Frontend: /admin/users page renders AdminUsersPage with data-testid="admin-users-page" + all spec'd testids (admin-users-add-btn, admin-users-search, admin-users-status-filter, nav-admin-users), user rows populate after PIN step-up (verified visually in screenshot — multiple Reg Test / Scenario User rows), deprecated footer shows 3× /roles + 2× /permissions + 2× /access-review links and 'Advanced' label occurrences x3. Create User dialog opens with data-testid="create-user-dialog"; Step 1 has create-user-name/-email/-password/-next; Next is correctly disabled on invalid email + short password; Step 2 renders with built-in common roles (Billing Specialist, Clinic Manager, Compliance Officer, Front Desk, Medical Assistant, Org Owner, Patient Portal User, Provider, Read-Only Auditor) and super_admin / integration_account are NOT in the visible list pre-toggle (correct hide behavior). Static grep in CreateUserDialog.jsx confirms all spec'd testids are wired: create-user-role-{role.key} (L408), create-user-toggle-advanced-roles (L246), create-user-effective-summary (L297), create-user-back/-next/-submit. Nav item and deprecated routes didn't fully complete in-browser due to chained PIN step-up gates for role.read on each new /api/authz/roles + /api/authz/users/{id}/effective-permissions call — this is expected HIPAA-hardening behavior; not a regression.

integration_issues.flow: Create User Step 2 (roles list) + Edit Access dialog (effective-permissions)
integration_issues.issue: Each of /api/authz/roles (role.read) and /api/authz/users/{id}/effective-permissions (user.read) triggers its own PIN step-up prompt because the admin test account has a Security PIN enrolled. Functionally correct and consistent with HIPAA hardening; however from an admin UX perspective the CreateUserDialog fires /authz/roles on mount, which means opening 'Add user' shows a reauth modal BEFORE the user has even typed anything. Consider lazy-loading /authz/roles only on transition to step 2, OR issue a single reauth for the whole dialog session to avoid double step-ups (step 2 + step 3 preview).

action_items: (Optional UX polish) Defer the /api/authz/roles fetch in CreateUserDialog until the user actually advances to Step 2, so opening 'Add user' doesn't immediately prompt a PIN step-up at Step 1.

(Optional) Leftover custom role 'InUse Test 71b6c5' from Phase 2 tests is visible in the role picker — harmless seeded test data; can be cleaned up via DELETE /api/authz/roles/in_use_test_71b6c5?force=true.
critical_code_review_comments: CreateUserDialog.jsx testid coverage is complete and matches spec (grep confirms create-user-role-${role.key} at L408, create-user-toggle-advanced-roles at L246, create-user-effective-summary at L297, -back/-next/-submit all present).

Phase 2 tests assign custom roles to the admin user itself to exercise the in-use guard (test_delete_in_use_role_requires_force + test_list_roles_user_count_reflects_assignment). This is flagged in the tests as 'not great practice but sufficient' — fine for testing, but note that an unclean teardown would leave the admin with stray custom roles. Current _cleanup_role uses force=true so the cleanup unassigns the role atomically — OK.

Permission catalog sort: rank map uses {critical,high,medium,low,none} — ensure constants.PERMISSIONS never emits a sensitivity outside this set, otherwise sort falls to rank 99 and still passes the monotonic check but would yield nondeterministic ordering.

explain_permissions summary string starts with 'This user can' (asserted in test_effective_permissions_admin_view_with_explanation) — stable contract; future localization work should keep this prefix or extend tests.

Role list shape: test_list_roles_includes_is_custom_and_user_counts asserts >= 11 baselines. With lingering custom roles from prior test runs, the list will grow over time. Consider a periodic cleanup job for custom roles prefixed 'Test Role ', 'Patch Test ', 'Delete Test ', 'CountTest ', 'InUse Test ', 'Filter Test ', 'Empty Patch ', 'Cloned Front Desk ' if preview env tests are run frequently.

success_rate.frontend: ~90% — all AdminUsersPage + Create dialog Step-1 + Step-2 role-hide behavior + deprecated footer + nav testids verified visually and via DOM probes; deeper interactive Step-2 / Step-3 / Edit-dialog flows were gated by repeated PIN step-up prompts. Static grep confirms remaining testids are present in code.

test_credentials: admin@ccms.app / Admin@ComplianceClinic1 — admin has PIN enrolled, use 'Use password instead' link in reauth dialog.
seed_data_creation: None new. A leftover 'InUse Test 71b6c5' custom role from previous Phase 2 test run is present but harmless.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend Phase 1+2 endpoints are stable — test_permission_catalog_phase1.py (12) and test_custom_roles_phase2.py (14) are the canonical regression suites. Run them via `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_permission_catalog_phase1.py /app/backend/tests/test_custom_roles_phase2.py -v`. For frontend coverage on the admin test account, every /api/authz call for role.read/user.read triggers a PIN reauth dialog (curly apostrophe 'Confirm it’s you'). Handle via `page.get_by_text('Use password instead', exact=True).click()` then fill password in [role='dialog'] input[type='password']. If the UI under test does many /authz calls in quick succession (CreateUserDialog mounts /authz/roles; step 3 fires /authz/roles/preview-effective-permissions; EditUserAccessDialog fires /authz/users/{id}/effective-permissions + /authz/roles), each will prompt separately — budget extra waits. All expected testids are wired in CreateUserDialog.jsx (lines 158,177,187,200,210,246,297,339,348,359,408), AdminUsersPage (admin-users-page/-add-btn/-search/-status-filter + admin-user-row-{id}/-edit-{id}/-disable-{id}/-enable-{id}), navConfig (nav-admin-users). Phase 4 (grouped Role Editor) + Phase 5 (migration) are NOT yet implemented — do not try to test them.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs. The only friction (chained PIN step-ups in CreateUserDialog) is a design-level UX observation, not a defect.
